rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF BESSIE WRIGHT VS. FIRST MORTGAGE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 1, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANTS CITIMORTGAGE, INC., MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEM, INC., CR TITLE SERVICES INC., AS TRUSTEE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (Violation of CC§2923.5) is overruled. Defendant can bring a Summary Judgment Motion is in fact Plaintiff does not have an ownership interest as alleged in the complaint. Also, Plaintiff has alleged that Defendant did not comply with the counseling requirement. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (breach of contract) is sustained with leave to amend. There are not allegations to support the cause of action. Further, Plaintiff should plead the breach of contract and fraud causes of action separately. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (Violation of CC§2932.5) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff lacks standing because it is not a holder of the note in due course. See Ferguson v. Avelo Mortgage, LLC (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1618. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 4th cause of action (Violation of CC§2934) is sustained without leave to amend. Defendant correctly notes that CC2934 provides that an assignment ¿may¿ be recorded and Plaintiff¿s reliance on 2934a does not apply to this case. Further, the substitution of trustee that was recorded is attached to the Amended Complaint. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 5th cause of action (Violation of CC§1572) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff attempts to plead fraud but there are no facts provided to support the cause of action. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (UCL) is sustained with leave to amend. The cause of action should expressly identify what statutes were violated. Further, Plaintiff has standing to maintain this cause of action. Plaintiff does not need to wait for foreclosure to occur to bring this cause of action. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 7th cause of action (Violation of CC§2941.9) is sustained witho

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 1, 2011, line 6, PLAINTIFF TOGAST, LLC Motion To Seal. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TOGAST, LLC VS. LEVI STRAUSS & CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 1, 2011, line 6, PLAINTIFF TOGAST, LLC Motion For Preliminary Injunction. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. EVEN IF PLAINTIFF WERE TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS, MONEY DAMAGES PROVIDE AN ADEQUATE REMEDY. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: AVIGNON JULIAN LLC VS. JOANN DANN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 1, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANT JOANN DANN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. rulings.Case Title: HONG SANG MARKET, INC. VS. VIVIEN PENG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 1, 2011, line 10, DEFENDANT VIVIEN PENG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. CCP 430.10(c) IS APPLICABLE WHERE THE SAME CAUSE OF ACTION IS INVOLVED AND THE PARTIES ARE ALIGNED IN THE SAME MANNER. BUSH V. SUP. CRT. (1992) 10 CAL.APP.4TH 1374, 1384. =(302/LMG)
